title: Threads and processes deterministic draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and no code task for Threads and processes; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Threads and processes: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Python: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Processes share ordinary mutable objects automatically, and threads cannot have races because of the interpreter lock.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Threads share mutable state and require coordination; processes use isolated memory and explicit communication, so choose after measuring workload and transfer costs.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Threads share mutable state and require coordination; processes use isolated memory and explicit communication, so choose after measuring workload and transfer costs.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Threads share mutable state and require coordination; processes use isolated memory and explicit communication, so choose after measuring workload and transfer costs.
Processes share ordinary mutable objects automatically, and threads cannot have races because of the interpreter lock.

answer.explanation: Threads share mutable state and require coordination; processes use isolated memory and explicit communication, so choose after measuring workload and transfer costs.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'isolated memory'. Threads share mutable state and require coordination; processes use isolated memory and explicit communication, so choose after measuring workload and transfer costs.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the exact language rule. Threads share mutable state and require coordination; processes use isolated memory and explicit communication, so choose after measuring workload and transfer costs.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical checkpoint phrase that completes the source-aligned correction.
